content.observe: A process can be one line of execution, or it can split itself into several — threads, sharing the same memory but moving independently.
content.fault: You tried to do three things at once as a single thread. You could only ever do one. Splitting was the answer, and you didn't split.

hotspots.label: Single-Threaded Process
hotspots.detail: One program counter, one stack — strictly one thing happening at a time.

hotspots.label: Multi-Threaded Process
hotspots.detail: Multiple threads, each with its own stack and PC, sharing one address space.

hotspots.label: Many-to-One Model
hotspots.detail: Many user threads mapped onto a single kernel thread.

hotspots.label: One-to-One Model
hotspots.detail: Each user thread maps to its own kernel thread — true parallelism, more overhead.
